name: Facebook & Instagram
description: Meta Graph API operations for Facebook Pages and Instagram Business: page/post management, photo + video publishing, Instagram Reels containers, and social analytics. One OAuth grant covers both surfaces.

tools.name: post_to_page
tools.description: Post text, links, or photos to a Facebook page. For IMAGE/PHOTO posts, use the 'image' parameter with a public URL - do NOT use 'link' for images. The 'link' parameter is only for sharing webpage URLs. For video uploads, use facebook-api-post-video instead. Caller MUST also include access_token=<page-level token from list_pages> — Facebook rejects the user token for /feed writes (error 210).

message.description: Post message content (used as caption for photos)

image.description: Image/photo URL to post (must be publicly accessible). Use this for posting images - NOT the 'link' parameter.

link.description: Webpage URL to share (NOT for images - use 'image' parameter for photos)

photos.description: Array of photo URLs for multi-image posts (must be publicly accessible)

access_token.description: Page-level access token (from list_pages → data[].access_token). Required: Facebook rejects user-level tokens for /feed writes.

tools.name: post_photo_to_page
tools.description: Upload and post a photo to a Facebook page using a publicly accessible image URL. Use this (not post_to_page) whenever you have an image — /feed only renders link previews and ignores image fields. Caller MUST include access_token=<page-level token from list_pages>.

url.description: Publicly accessible image URL (Graph API will fetch + attach).

caption.description: Caption text for the photo (rendered as the post body).

published.description: Whether the photo is published immediately (true) or staged (false). Defaults to true.

access_token.description: Page-level access token (from list_pages → data[].access_token). Required.

tools.name: post_video
tools.description: Upload and post a video to a Facebook page using a publicly accessible URL. Videos must be under 1GB and 20 minutes. Caller MUST include access_token=<page-level token from list_pages> — Facebook rejects user tokens for /videos writes (error 210).

file_url.description: Publicly accessible URL to the video file (MP4, MOV, AVI, WMV, FLV supported)

access_token.description: Page-level access token (from list_pages → data[].access_token). Required: Facebook rejects user-level tokens for /videos writes.

tools.name: get_page_posts
tools.description: List posts from a Facebook page. Calls GET /{pageId}/posts on the Graph API. Page-level access_token is required (user-level tokens are rejected for /posts on Pages). Returns posts with message content, images (full_picture URL), permalink_url, and engagement metrics (reactions/comments/shares.summary.total_count when included in fields).

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include

since.description: Unix timestamp or date string to filter posts from

until.description: Unix timestamp or date string to filter posts until

tools.name: get_page_insights
tools.description: Get analytics data for a Facebook page

tools.name: list_pages
tools.description: List all Facebook pages managed by the authenticated user with details like name, category, fan count, and verification status

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include

tools.name: facebook_get_post
tools.description: Get a Facebook post by id, including engagement counts when fields are requested. Calls GET /{postId}?fields=... on the Graph API; postId must be the full pageId_postId format. Page-level access_token is required (user-level tokens are rejected with error 210, same as the write endpoints). Common fields for metrics: 'likes.summary(true),comments.summary(true),shares,reactions.summary(true)' — counts come back under the .summary.total_count of each.

postId.description: Facebook post ID in pageId_postId format

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields. For engagement counts use 'likes.summary(true),comments.summary(true),shares,reactions.summary(true)'.

access_token.description: Page-level access token (from list_pages → data[].access_token). Required for posts owned by Pages.

tools.name: facebook_update_post
tools.description: Edit the message of a Facebook post. Calls POST /{postId} on the Graph API; postId is the full pageId_postId format. Page-level access_token required (user-level tokens are rejected with error 210, same as the write endpoints).

postId.description: Facebook post ID in pageId_postId format

message.description: New message body for the post

tools.name: facebook_delete_post
tools.description: Permanently delete a Facebook post from a page. Calls DELETE /{postId} on the Graph API; postId must be the full pageId_postId format that post_to_page / post_photo_to_page / post_video return as `id`. Page-level access_token is required (user-level tokens are rejected with error 210, same as the write endpoints).

postId.description: Facebook post ID in pageId_postId format (the `id` value returned by the post_to_page family).

access_token.description: Page-level access token (from list_pages → data[].access_token). Required: Facebook rejects user-level tokens for delete on page posts.

tools.name: create_media_container
tools.description: Instagram step 1 of 2: create a media container with image/video URL and caption. Returns containerId for step 2 (publish_media_container). For videos, sync=true (default) waits for processing to complete. The IG Business account must be linked to a Facebook Page that this connection has access to (instagramAccountId comes from list_pages with fields=...,instagram_business_account{id}).

instagramAccountId.description: Instagram Business Account ID (from the linked FB Page's instagram_business_account.id)

image_url.description: URL of image to post (for IMAGE/CAROUSEL). Snake_case is canonical per Graph API.

video_url.description: URL of video to post (for REELS). Snake_case is canonical per Graph API.

media_type.description: Type of media (VIDEO is deprecated, use REELS for videos)

sync.description: For videos/reels: wait for processing before returning. Default true.

access_token.description: Page-level access token (from list_pages → data[].access_token of the FB Page that owns this IG account). Required for IG Business writes.

tools.name: get_container_status
tools.description: Poll an Instagram media container's processing status before publishing. Returns {id, status_code, status}. status_code values: FINISHED (ready to publish), IN_PROGRESS (still processing — Reels especially take time), ERROR (transcode/validation failed), EXPIRED (container older than 24h, must recreate). Calls GET /{containerId}?fields=status_code,status on the Graph API.

access_token.description: Page-level access token (the same one used to create the container).

tools.name: publish_media_container
tools.description: Instagram step 2 of 2: publish the container created in step 1. Returns the published media id.

creation_id.description: Alias for containerId — Graph API expects this field name.

access_token.description: Page-level access token (from list_pages). Required for IG Business writes.

tools.name: get_media_insights
tools.description: Fetch Instagram Business media insights (per-post metrics). Calls GET /{mediaId}/insights?metric=...&access_token=PAGE_TOKEN on the Graph API. Returns {data: [{name, period, values: [{value: N}]}]} — one entry per requested metric. Common metrics for FEED posts: reach, likes, comments, saves, shares (where supported). Metric availability varies by media type — REELS exposes plays, IG VIDEO ones differ.

mediaId.description: IG media id — the platform_post_id returned by publish_media_container.

metric.description: Comma-separated metric names (no spaces). Default covers the engagement basics.

access_token.description: Page-level access token (the same token that owns the IG Business account).

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send an Instagram DM, sender action (typing/seen), reaction, or private reply to a comment. Single endpoint POST /{instagramAccountId}/messages — body shape switches by intent. (1) Text DM: recipient={id:IGSID}, message={text:'...'}. (2) Media DM: recipient={id:IGSID}, message={attachment:{type:'image'|'video'|'audio'|'file', payload:{url, is_reusable?}}}. (3) Quick replies: message={text, quick_replies:[{content_type:'text', title, payload}]}. (4) Sender action: recipient={id:IGSID}, sender_action='typing_on'|'typing_off'|'mark_seen'. (5) Reaction: recipient={id:IGSID}, sender_action='react'|'unreact', payload={message_id, reaction:'love'}. (6) Private reply to comment: recipient={comment_id:'...'}, message={text:'...'} — 7-day single-use window from the comment, the only way to initiate to a user who hasn't DM'd you. Standard recipients are bound by Meta's 24-hour messaging window unless using an approved message tag. Returns {recipient_id, message_id} for messages; {recipient_id} for sender actions. Page-level access_token required.

instagramAccountId.description: Instagram Business Account ID (sender). From list_pages → data[].instagram_business_account.id.

recipient.description: Who receives the message. Use {id: '<IGSID>'} for a known Instagram-scoped user (obtained from an inbound webhook event or list_conversations participants). Use {comment_id: '<comment-id>'} for a private reply to a public comment (7-day window from the comment, single-use).

id.description: Instagram-scoped user id (IGSID) of the recipient.

comment_id.description: Comment id to private-reply to (alternative to id). Single-use, 7-day window.

message.description: Message payload. Omit when sending a sender_action. One of {text}, {attachment}, or {text, quick_replies}.

is_reusable.description: Cache the attachment server-side so it can be reused by id in later sends.

quick_replies.description: Tap-to-reply chips shown beneath the message. Max 13.

payload.description: Opaque value echoed back in the inbound webhook when tapped.

sender_action.description: Use instead of `message` to send a typing indicator, read receipt, or reaction. typing_on/typing_off/mark_seen require no payload; react/unreact require the `payload` field below.

payload.description: Reaction payload — only when sender_action is 'react' or 'unreact'.

message_id.description: The inbound message id being reacted to.

access_token.description: Page-level access token (from list_pages → data[].access_token of the FB Page that owns this IG account). Required.

tools.name: list_conversations
tools.description: List Instagram DM conversations (threads) for an IG Business account. Calls GET /{instagramAccountId}/conversations?platform=instagram on the Graph API. Returns {data:[{id, updated_time, participants:{data:[{id, username}]}}], paging:{cursors, next}}. Pass the next cursor's `after` value to page. Use this to find IGSIDs of users you can message (those who have messaged you) and to drive a conversation list UI.

instagramAccountId.description: Instagram Business Account ID (your account, not the participant).

platform.description: Must be 'instagram' to scope to IG DMs (vs. Messenger).

fields.description: Comma-separated fields. Default surfaces participants inline so callers don't need a second roundtrip.

user_id.description: Optional IGSID filter — return only the conversation with this participant (if it exists).

limit.description: Page size (Meta caps around 25 by default).

after.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's paging.cursors.after.

tools.name: get_conversation
tools.description: Fetch a single Instagram DM conversation with its messages inline. Calls GET /{conversationId}?fields=... on the Graph API. Default fields pull the most recent 20 messages with sender/recipient/body/attachments — Meta caps inline message history at ~20; for older messages you must paginate via the messages edge separately.

fields.description: Fields selector. Default expands the messages edge with the common per-message fields.

tools.name: get_message
tools.description: Fetch a single Instagram DM message by id, including attachments and reactions. Calls GET /{messageId}?fields=... on the Graph API. Useful for fetching attachment URLs or reaction details that aren't included in the lightweight conversation listing.

messageId.description: Message id (from a conversation's messages edge or an inbound webhook event).
